channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: This Fake Gym for the Unemployed Might Be the Most Accurate AI Satire Yet
item.description: The viral fake ad about harvesting human energy hits too close to home.

item.title: How In-App Browsers Shape Measurement and Experience
item.description: The browser divide means having two internets but one customer.

item.title: AI is the new workplace issue dividing managers and employees
item.description: As companies ramp up pressure on their workers to embrace artificial intelligence , a new source of workplace conflict has emerged. A report released ...

item.title: Kroger is closing stores: See the updated list that shows shuttered locations across the country
item.description: Groceries are a little harder to come by in dozens of neighborhoods this year in the wake of an ongoing retreat from The Kroger Co. The Cincinnati-bas...

item.title: February was the biggest month in venture history, thanks only to OpenAI, Anthropic, and Waymo
item.description: Startup funding surged to a historic $189 billion in February, driven almost entirely by three AI mega-rounds that captured 83% of the total.

item.title: The U.S. just unexpectedly lost 92,000 jobs. Here’s how that could affect Fed interest rates, gas prices, and the Iran war
item.description: The latest U.S. jobs report is out, and it isn’t pretty. The economy lost 92,000 jobs in February , missing expectations, as unemployment rose to 4.4%...

item.title: Ozempic’s Key Ingredient Could Be Produced for Just $3 a Month. What Does That Mean for Patients?
item.description: The research could open the door to worldwide access to the drug.

item.title: A New Trial Reveals Disturbing Data on Child Safety at Meta—and Zuckerberg’s Response
item.description: During testimony in a New Mexico trial, Zuckerberg said platforms used by billions can never be “perfect,” as prosecutors argue Meta knowingly allowed...

item.title: A Minneapolis Fed report details how much Trump’s immigration crackdown hurt businesses and workers. ‘There are not any people to hire’
item.description: "We are hiring now to replace these workers or get more reliable alternatives, but there are not any people to hire."

item.title: McDonald’s, ‘Burgergate,’ pile-ons, and what it all means for brands moving forward
item.description: By now, anyone who follows major brands has seen it or heard of it: The small bite that went ‘round the world. McDonald’s CEO and chairman Chris Kempc...

item.title: Nearly 1,000 workers laid off at SK Battery plant in Georgia as companies cancel EVs and Trump Admin eliminates auto company incentives
item.description: Ford announced it was cancelling the all-electric Ford F-150 Lightning in December. SK Battery America Inc., a supplier of the Ford truck, laid off ne...

item.title: Jet fuel prices just jumped 80%. Will airline tickets get more expensive next?
item.description: Get ready to pay more to fly. The war in Iran has sent fuel prices surging. On Friday, spot prices for jet fuel were nearly $4 per gallon, up roughly ...

item.title: Silicon Valley investor Vinod Khosla predicts education will be free, and the future of college ‘is a real question’
item.description: The billionaire VC says AI raises questions about how we value expertise.

item.title: OpenAI investor Vinod Khosla believes AI will be able to do 80% of all jobs by 2030. Here’s how life could be affordable after mass unemployment
item.description: The Silicon Valley billionaire laid out a vision to Fortune of a deflationary economy—but also free healthcare and education, along with a tax overhau...

item.title: Context: The Competitive Advantage Global Teams Can’t Ignore
item.description: Context may be leaders’ most underleveraged strategic asset.
